response:
*KEEP THE AREA CLEAN*  
BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY TWICE A DAY WITH A SOFT-BRISTLED TOOTHBRUSH.  
RINSE WITH WATER AFTER EVERY MEAL TO REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES.  
*FLOSS CAREFULLY*  
FLOSS AROUND THE AFFECTED TOOTH TO REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES.  
*AVOID CERTAIN FOODS*  
STAY AWAY FROM HARD, STICKY, OR CHEWWY FOODS.  
*USE TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS*  
TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS CAN BE USED TO FILL THE VOID.  
*PAIN MANAGEMENT*  
USE OVER-THE-COUNTER PAIN RELIEVERS LIKE IBUPROFEN.  
*SAINT WATER RINSE*  
RINSE WITH A SALT WATER SOLUTION TO REDUCE INFLAMMATION.  
*AVOID EXTREME TEMPERATURES*  
CHEW ON THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF YOUR MOUTH TO AVOID PRESSURE.  
*MONITOR FOR CHANGES*  
KEEP AN EYE ON THE TOOTH FOR ANY CHANGES OR INFECTION.  
*SEE DENTAL CARE*  
LOOK FOR DENTAL CARE OPTIONS IN YOUR CURRENT LOCATION.  
*DENTAL EMERGENCIES*  
SEEK DENTAL TREATMENT IMMEDIATELY IF YOU DEVELOP AN ABSCESS.  
*BOX*  
BOXES CAN BE USED TO PROTECT THE TOOTH TEMPORARILY.  
*CAR*  
CARDS CAN BE USED TO HELP WITH PAIN RELIEF.  
*TRADITION*  
TRADITIONS CAN HELP IN PREVENTING COMPLICATIONS.  
*TWO*  
TWO TIMES A DAY IS RECOMMENDED FOR CLEANING.
